# XCTWaiter

**Class**

Waits for the fulfillment of a group of expectations.

## Declaration

```swift
class XCTWaiter
```

## Overview

You can use waiters with or without a delegate to respond to events such as completion, timeout, or invalid expectation fulfillment. [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTestCase] automatically conforms to the [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTWaiterDelegate] protocol and automatically reports timeouts and other unexpected events as test failures.

You can use waiters without a delegate or any association with a test case instance. This allows test support libraries to provide convenience methods for waiting without having to pass test cases through those APIs.

## Creating a Waiter

- **init(delegate:)**: Creates a new waiter with the specified delegate.

## Waiting for Expectations

- **fulfillment(of:timeout:enforceOrder:)**: Waits on a group of expectations for up to the specified timeout, optionally enforcing their order of fulfillment.
- **wait(for:)**: Waits on a group of expectations.
- **wait(for:enforceOrder:)**: Waits on a group of expectations optionally enforcing their order of fulfillment.
- **wait(for:timeout:)**: Waits on a group of expectations for up to the specified timeout.
- **wait(for:timeout:enforceOrder:)**: Waits on a group of expectations for up to the specified timeout, optionally enforcing their order of fulfillment.
- **fulfillment(of:timeout:enforceOrder:)**: Creates a waiter that waits on group of expectations for up to the specified timeout, optionally enforcing their order of fulfillment.
- **wait(for:)**: Creates a waiter that waits on a group of expectations.
- **wait(for:enforceOrder:)**: Creates a waiter that waits on a group of expectations optionally enforcing their order of fulfillment.
- **wait(for:timeout:)**: Creates a waiter that waits on a group of expectations for up to the specified timeout.
- **wait(for:timeout:enforceOrder:)**: Creates a waiter that waits on a group of expectations for up to the specified timeout, optionally enforcing their order of fulfillment.
- **XCTWaiter.Result**: Result states returned by a waiter when it completes, times out, fails, or is interrupted.

## Responding to Expectation Fulfilment

- **delegate**: The delegate to which expectation fulfillment events will be reported.
- **XCTWaiterDelegate**: Defines methods that are called when [doc://com.apple.xctest/documentation/XCTest/XCTWaiter] expectations are fulfilled correctly or incorrectly.
- **fulfilledExpectations**: An array of expectations that were fulfilled, in order, up until the waiter stopped waiting.

## Inherits From

- NSObject

## Conforms To

- CVarArg
- CustomDebugStringConvertible
- CustomStringConvertible
- Equatable
- Hashable
- NSObjectProtocol
- Sendable
- SendableMetatype
